key_points: The Mughal Empire weakened due to weak rulers, internal conflicts, and administrative issues
Regional powers like the Marathas, Sikhs, Nizams, and Rajputs grew stronger
European trading companies, especially British and French, interfered in local politics
Alliances and rivalries among regional powers were common
Major battles like Plassey (1757) and Buxar (1764) were pivotal in shaping political power
examples: Marathas expanded across central and western India
British East India Company gained control of Bengal after the Battle of Plassey
Nizam of Hyderabad allied with Marathas and Europeans at times
Sikhs consolidated power in Punjab during declining Mughal control
formulas: Decline of Mughals → Rise of regional powers + European influence
Plassey/Buxar → British political and economic control
exam_tips: Remember key regional powers and their areas of influence
Know major battles and their outcomes
Understand the role of European companies in politics
Be able to explain alliances and rivalries
Use examples to support answers about political changes
